rules: All emails are personalized — no generic templates
Follow-ups only after 5-7 days if no reply

targets.name: The Mortgage Reports
targets.why_selected: Highest-scoring target (90/100). Leading mortgage education site — their entire audience is researching mortgages. Perfect content fit for an affordability widget. Has a tools section and resource pages.
targets.pitch_angle: Free, accurate tool their readers can use right on their mortgage guides — complements their existing educational content perfectly.

targets.name: Money Under 30
targets.why_selected: Young adult personal finance blog — their readers are at prime first-home-buying age. Homebuying is a core topic. Clear contact page. Approachable site with history of featuring useful tools.
targets.pitch_angle: Free widget that helps young adults figure out their homebuying budget right on Money Under 30's guides.

targets.name: Maximum Real Estate Exposure
targets.why_selected: Active real estate blog by an experienced agent. Directly relevant audience (homebuyers). Has resource pages. Personal/small-site feel means higher chance of direct reply. Contact page is clear and personal.
targets.pitch_angle: Practical, free tool for their homebuyer readers — fits naturally with their existing guides.

targets.name: NeighborWorks America
targets.why_selected: National nonprofit providing homebuyer education and housing counseling. .org domain with strong authority. Calculator directly supports their mission. Contact page available. Nonprofit angle opens doors.
targets.pitch_angle: Free public resource tool for their housing counselors and the families they serve — mission-aligned.

targets.name: Dough Roller
targets.why_selected: Established personal finance blog with an existing tools section and resource pages. Has mortgage/real estate content. Clear contact method. Established site with track record of featuring useful tools — natural fit.
targets.pitch_angle: Free addition to their existing tools section — complements their mortgage content.

emails.subject: Free mortgage affordability widget for The Mortgage Reports
emails.body:
Hi,

I've been reading The Mortgage Reports for a while — your coverage of rates, affordability, and homebuying strategies is some of the most practical mortgage education out there. I built something I think would fit naturally with your content.

It's a free, embeddable mortgage affordability calculator. Readers input their income, debts, and down payment — and instantly see their affordable home price, max loan amount, and full DTI breakdown using standard 28%/36% lending guidelines.

You can see it live here:
https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed

If you think your readers would find it useful, the embed code is:
<iframe src="https://www.qfinhub.com/embed/mortgage-affordability" width="100%" height="650" style="border:0;max-width:100%;" loading="lazy"></iframe>

It's 100% free — no signup required, no user data collected, and it works on mobile. I built it as a genuinely useful free tool.

Would this be helpful for your readers? Happy to answer any questions.

Best,
Qasem Mohammed
Founder, QFINHUB
https://www.qfinhub.com

emails.subject: Free tool: help your readers figure out their homebuying budget
emails.body:
Hi,

I've been a reader of Money Under 30 for a while — your homebuying guides are some of the most practical I've seen for young adults figuring this stuff out for the first time. That's actually why I'm writing.

I built a free mortgage affordability calculator widget that I think your readers would find genuinely useful. They can plug in their income, debts, and down payment, and instantly see how much house they can afford — using the standard 28%/36% DTI ratios that lenders actually use.

emails.subject: Free tool for your homebuyer education program
emails.body:
Hi,

I've been following NeighborWorks America's work in housing counseling and homebuyer education — the impact you have on families and communities is remarkable. I wanted to share a free tool that might support your counselors and the people they serve.

It's a mortgage affordability calculator widget. Homebuyers can adjust their income, debts, and down payment and see in real time what they can afford using standard 28%/36% DTI lending guidelines. Counselors can use it during sessions, and clients can continue using it at home afterward.

Live demo:
https://www.qfinhub.com/widgets/mortgage-affordability-embed

The embed code is straightforward:
<iframe src="https://www.qfinhub.com/embed/mortgage-affordability" width="100%" height="650" style="border:0;max-width:100%;" loading="lazy"></iframe>

Key details: 100% free, no signup, no personal data collected — all calculations happen in the browser. No ads, no upsell. I built this as a public resource because I believe practical financial tools should be freely available.

Would this be useful for your program? I'd be happy to help with any questions.

Thank you for the important work you do,
Qasem Mohammed
Founder, QFINHUB
https://www.qfinhub.com
